document_metadata.title: Emmerdale Ray Walters' secret parent 'exposed' and they're a village resident
document_metadata.first_words: Emmerdale fans believe they've exposed Ray Walters' secret parent, and they're a village resident. Drug dealer Ray (Joe Absolom) made his first appearance in the Dales earlier this year when Mackenzie Boyd (Lawrence Robb) contacted him about buying Lewis Barton's (Bradley Riches) cannabis crop. Robert Sugden (Ryan Hawley) then covertly stole the drugs and sold them to Ray, using the proceeds to buy his grandmother Annie's (Sheila Mercier) field. It later came to light that Ray was involved with Dylan Penders (Fred Kettle), having persuaded the youngster to break into Butler's

chunks.content: Drug dealer Ray (Joe Absolom) made his first appearance in the Dales earlier this year when Mackenzie Boyd (Lawrence Robb) contacted him about buying Lewis Barton's (Bradley Riches) cannabis crop. Robert Sugden (Ryan Hawley) then covertly stole the drugs and sold them to Ray, using the proceeds to buy his grandmother Annie's (Sheila Mercier) field. It later came to light that Ray was involved with Dylan Penders (Fred Kettle), having persuaded the youngster to break into Butler's Farm to survey the property. However, the scheme went disastrously wrong when a scared Kyle Winchester (Huey Quinn) stabbed Dylan in the leg with a pitchfork, reports the Daily Star.
chunks.content_original: Drug dealer Ray (Joe Absolom) made his first appearance in the Dales earlier this year when Mackenzie Boyd (Lawrence Robb) contacted him about buying Lewis Barton's (Bradley Riches) cannabis crop. Robert Sugden (Ryan Hawley) then covertly stole the drugs and sold them to Ray, using the proceeds to buy his grandmother Annie's (Sheila Mercier) field. It later came to light that Ray was involved with Dylan Penders (Fred Kettle), having persuaded the youngster to break into Butler's Farm to survey the property. However, the scheme went disastrously wrong when a scared Kyle Winchester (Huey Quinn) stabbed Dylan in the leg with a pitchfork, reports the Daily Star.

chunks.content: Now, Ray has entangled himself with April Windsor (Amelia Flanagan), who is frantically trying to find Dylan a place to stay after Paddy Kirk (Dominic Brunt) and Mandy Dingle (Lisa Riley) evicted him last week. On Monday (1 September) evening, Ray coerced Dylan into undertaking a perilous job, with April stepping up to take on the task instead. Upon her arrival at the car park for the drug drop, a hooded man demanded she hand over the bag before brandishing a knife. Dylan comforted a shaken April, who was compelled to give the drugs to the stranger, before it was revealed that Ray had masterminded the entire encounter.
chunks.content_original: Now, Ray has entangled himself with April Windsor (Amelia Flanagan), who is frantically trying to find Dylan a place to stay after Paddy Kirk (Dominic Brunt) and Mandy Dingle (Lisa Riley) evicted him last week. On Monday (1 September) evening, Ray coerced Dylan into undertaking a perilous job, with April stepping up to take on the task instead. Upon her arrival at the car park for the drug drop, a hooded man demanded she hand over the bag before brandishing a knife. Dylan comforted a shaken April, who was compelled to give the drugs to the stranger, before it was revealed that Ray had masterminded the entire encounter.

chunks.content: Now, numerous Emmerdale viewers reckon that Ray might be set for a reunion with a long-lost relative in forthcoming episodes, with many theorising that Celia Daniels (Jaye Griffiths) could be his mother. This follows Celia's recent heart-to-heart with Moira Dingle (Natalie J Robb), where she revealed she's lost touch with her grown-up son. “[I have] a son whom I barely get to see, which is appropriate. He's a grown man with his own life; he shouldn't have to worry about me,“ she said. “My husband died a few years ago... They say it's supposed to get easier with time. [The pain] dims a little, then pops up when you least expect it.“
chunks.content_original: Now, numerous Emmerdale viewers reckon that Ray might be set for a reunion with a long-lost relative in forthcoming episodes, with many theorising that Celia Daniels (Jaye Griffiths) could be his mother. This follows Celia's recent heart-to-heart with Moira Dingle (Natalie J Robb), where she revealed she's lost touch with her grown-up son. “[I have] a son whom I barely get to see, which is appropriate. He's a grown man with his own life; he shouldn't have to worry about me,“ she said. “My husband died a few years ago... They say it's supposed to get easier with time. [The pain] dims a little, then pops up when you least expect it.“

chunks.content: Regarding the possibility of Ray being Celia's offspring, one viewer posted on a Facebook fan page: “Wasn't it Ray who got Dylan to break into the farm when he got stabbed with that fork. Celia was working for Kim, maybe she asked Ray (her son maybe) to arrange it?“ Another chimed in: “Yesss they must be related,“ whilst a third commented: “Ooo I could totally see this being revealed.“

raw_content:
Home - YorkshireLive Online https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/ RSS feed from YorkshireLive - Home Emmerdale Ray Walters' secret parent 'exposed' and they're a village resident https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/news/tv/emmerdale-ray-walters-secret-parent-32395678 Tue, 2 Sep 2025 10:30:00 +0000 Emmerdale fans are convinced that they have worked out the identity of Ray Walters' long-lost mother Tue, 02 Sep 2025 10:25:05 GMT 32395678 Sara Baalla 2025-09-02T10:25:05Z Emmerdale fans believe they've exposed Ray Walters' secret parent, and they're a village resident.

Drug dealer Ray (Joe Absolom) made his first appearance in the Dales earlier this year when Mackenzie Boyd (Lawrence Robb) contacted him about buying Lewis Barton's (Bradley Riches) cannabis crop.

Robert Sugden (Ryan Hawley) then covertly stole the drugs and sold them to Ray, using the proceeds to buy his grandmother Annie's (Sheila Mercier) field.

It later came to light that Ray was involved with Dylan Penders (Fred Kettle), having persuaded the youngster to break into Butler's Farm to survey the property.

However, the scheme went disastrously wrong when a scared Kyle Winchester (Huey Quinn) stabbed Dylan in the leg with a pitchfork, reports the Daily Star.

READ MORE: Emmerdale spoilers tease 'ultimate betrayal' in new Charity Dingle pregnancy twist

READ MORE: ITV Coronation Street legend 'set to kill' John Sugden in Emmerdale 'crossover'

Now, Ray has entangled himself with April Windsor (Amelia Flanagan), who is frantically trying to find Dylan a place to stay after Paddy Kirk (Dominic Brunt) and Mandy Dingle (Lisa Riley) evicted him last week.

On Monday (1 September) evening, Ray coerced Dylan into undertaking a perilous job, with April stepping up to take on the task instead.

Upon her arrival at the car park for the drug drop, a hooded man demanded she hand over the bag before brandishing a knife.

Dylan comforted a shaken April, who was compelled to give the drugs to the stranger, before it was revealed that Ray had masterminded the entire encounter.

Now, numerous Emmerdale viewers reckon that Ray might be set for a reunion with a long-lost relative in forthcoming episodes, with many theorising that Celia Daniels (Jaye Griffiths) could be his mother.

This follows Celia's recent heart-to-heart with Moira Dingle (Natalie J Robb), where she revealed she's lost touch with her grown-up son.

"[I have] a son whom I barely get to see, which is appropriate. He's a grown man with his own life; he shouldn't have to worry about me," she said.

"My husband died a few years ago... They say it's supposed to get easier with time. [The pain] dims a little, then pops up when you least expect it."

Regarding the possibility of Ray being Celia's offspring, one viewer posted on a Facebook fan page: "Wasn't it Ray who got Dylan to break into the farm when he got stabbed with that fork. Celia was working for Kim, maybe she asked Ray (her son maybe) to arrange it?"

Another chimed in: "Yesss they must be related," whilst a third commented: "Ooo I could totally see this being revealed."

Emmerdale airs weeknights on ITV1 at 7.30pm and is available to stream from 7am on ITVX

markdown_content:
Emmerdale fans believe they've exposed Ray Walters' secret parent, and they're a village resident.
Drug dealer Ray (Joe Absolom) made his first appearance in the Dales earlier this year when Mackenzie Boyd (Lawrence Robb) contacted him about buying Lewis Barton's (Bradley Riches) cannabis crop.
Robert Sugden (Ryan Hawley) then covertly stole the drugs and sold them to Ray, using the proceeds to buy his grandmother Annie's (Sheila Mercier) field.
It later came to light that Ray was involved with Dylan Penders (Fred Kettle), having persuaded the youngster to break into Butler's Farm to survey the property.
However, the scheme went disastrously wrong when a scared Kyle Winchester (Huey Quinn) stabbed Dylan in the leg with a pitchfork, reports the Daily Star.
\*\*READ MORE:\*\* Emmerdale spoilers tease 'ultimate betrayal' in new Charity Dingle pregnancy twist
\*\*READ MORE:\*\* ITV Coronation Street legend 'set to kill' John Sugden in Emmerdale 'crossover'
Now, Ray has entangled himself with April Windsor (Amelia Flanagan), who is frantically trying to find Dylan a place to stay after Paddy Kirk (Dominic Brunt) and Mandy Dingle (Lisa Riley) evicted him last week.
On Monday (1 September) evening, Ray coerced Dylan into undertaking a perilous job, with April stepping up to take on the task instead.
Upon her arrival at the car park for the drug drop, a hooded man demanded she hand over the bag before brandishing a knife.
Dylan comforted a shaken April, who was compelled to give the drugs to the stranger, before it was revealed that Ray had masterminded the entire encounter.
Now, numerous Emmerdale viewers reckon that Ray might be set for a reunion with a long-lost relative in forthcoming episodes, with many theorising that Celia Daniels (Jaye Griffiths) could be his mother.
This follows Celia's recent heart-to-heart with Moira Dingle (Natalie J Robb), where she revealed she's lost touch with her grown-up son.
"[I have] a son whom I barely get to see, which is appropriate. He's a grown man with his own life; he shouldn't have to worry about me," she said.
"My husband died a few years ago... They say it's supposed to get easier with time. [The pain] dims a little, then pops up when you least expect it."
Regarding the possibility of Ray being Celia's offspring, one viewer posted on a Facebook fan page: "Wasn't it Ray who got Dylan to break into the farm when he got stabbed with that fork. Celia was working for Kim, maybe she asked Ray (her son maybe) to arrange it?"
Another chimed in: "Yesss they must be related," whilst a third commented: "Ooo I could totally see this being revealed."
\*\*Emmerdale airs weeknights on ITV1 at 7.30pm and is available to stream from 7am on ITVX\*\*
